response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware/SoC expertise: AI accelerator architecture, SoC/IP modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no relevant experience in any of these areas, and the educational background (BS in CS) without advanced degrees or 8+ years in the specific hardware domain makes this a poor fit.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and embedded systems work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning workload optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN training/inference background
Career trajectory is full-stack web/product engineering, not hardware-software co-design at principal level

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture experience
Low-level systems and performance engineering background
